theory.intro: Multiplying or dividing by $10$, $100$ or $1000$ shifts every digit along the place-value columns. The decimal point appears to move.
facts: $\times 10$ moves each digit **one place left** (bigger); $\times 100$ moves it **two**; $\times 1000$ **three**.
Count the **zeros** in the power of $10$ — that is how many places to shift.
steps: Multiplying makes it bigger (shift left); dividing makes it smaller (shift right).
Move the decimal point that many places, adding zeros if needed.
